tactics: Opens combat with shortbow attacks from 60+ feet away, maximizing range advantage while staying out of melee
Uses Nimble Escape to disengage as a bonus action after each attack, maintaining distance and avoiding opportunity attacks—core to goblin hit-and-run strategy
Falls back behind cover, terrain, or allies when forced into melee, relying on AC 15 to weather hits while repositioning for ranged attacks
goblin.flee_threshold: Flees at ~50% HP (4 HP) or immediately if cornered with no escape route — goblins are cowardly opportunists who fight only when they have numerical advantage or a safe retreat path; a wounded goblin alone prioritizes survival over combat honor.

tactics: Maintains formation with allies, using shield and high AC (18) defensively while allowing ranged attackers to operate safely behind the line
Focuses fire on the most dangerous immediate threat (spellcasters or high-damage dealers) to eliminate threats before they can deal maximum damage
Uses the Parry reaction when available to reduce incoming damage, demonstrating tactical discipline and self-preservation instinct
Positions itself to protect weaker allies or control chokepoints, leveraging hobgoblin military discipline and coordinated tactics
hobgoblin.flee_threshold: Flees at ~25% HP (around 3 HP remaining) if the hobgoblin's unit is broken or will be routed — hobgoblin culture values organized retreat and tactical discipline over suicidal last stands, and a severely wounded hobgoblin would abandon a lost position to regroup with its warband.

tactics: Closes to melee range aggressively using its 30 ft. speed, relying on natural armor and HP pool to absorb damage while dealing consistent damage with its attacks
Uses its Beard ability to grapple and restrain enemies, locking down dangerous melee combatants or spellcasters to control the battlefield
Employs infernal resilience and damage resistance where applicable to outlast opponents in prolonged melee exchanges, forcing attrition in its favor
Positions itself to protect weaker allies or hold strategic ground, acting as a reliable infernal enforcer that doesn't need to retreat unless truly overwhelmed
bearded-devil.flee_threshold: Flees at ~25% HP (13 HP) if outmatched by multiple strong opponents or facing imminent death — devils are pragmatic servants of Hell and abandon losing battles to report to their masters or regroup, rather than waste themselves pointlessly.

tactics: Uses legendary actions and high mobility to stay away from multiple enemies; prefers to charm humanoids and turn them against the party rather than engage directly
Relies on Charm Person and hypnotic gaze to neutralize threats before combat escalates; concentrates on isolating single targets away from the group
Leverages regeneration (regains 20 HP at start of turn if has at least 1 HP) by withdrawing briefly to allow healing; uses Misty Step to reposition around difficult terrain and cover
Targets spellcasters and clerics first to prevent turn undead and dispel magic; uses children or charmed minions as shields and sources of blood to maintain survivability
vampire.flee_threshold: Flees at ~50% HP (72 HP) if significantly outnumbered, cornered, or exposed to sunlight — vampires are pragmatic hunters with centuries of survival instinct; a tactical retreat allows them to regroup, rebuild thrall networks, and strike when odds favor them. They rarely fight to final destruction.
